entity.title: SteerSpec Configuration
entity.description: The consumer-side configuration that declares which rules a project subscribes to and how they are enforced.

rules.body: IS a directory named `.strspc/` at the root of a project that consumes SteerSpec rules.
MAY contain a local cache for resolved rules and evaluation results.
MUST declare at least one rule source.
MAY reference a local Realm as a rule source, enabling a project to both author and consume rules.
MAY configure an evaluator for rule enforcement.
MAY declare fail conditions referencing rule lifecycle states.
Severity of a rule violation IS determined by the consumer configuration, not by the rule itself.

entity.title: Rule Source
entity.description: A rule source entry in config.yaml that declares where rules are fetched from and their applicability scope.

rules.body: IS an entry in the `rules` array of config.yaml.
MUST have a `source` property of type string.
MUST have a `scope` property with allowed values: global, local.

notes.content: Valid source formats: `github://SteerSpec/strspc-rules@v1/rules/core` (remote, version-pinned), `./rules/` (local Realm directory).
`global` means the source's rules apply to all files in the project. `local` means the rules apply only within the source's own directory tree. Scope is a hint for the evaluator, not a hard filter.

entity.title: Evaluator
entity.description: The evaluator configuration in config.yaml that determines which AI provider is used for rule enforcement.

rules.body: MUST have a `provider` property of type string.
MAY have an `endpoint` property of type string or null.

notes.content: Known providers: `claude` (Anthropic API), `openai` (OpenAI API), `ollama` (local model), `static-only` (no AI evaluation, structural checks only).
When null or absent, the evaluator uses the default endpoint for the chosen provider. Custom endpoints enable private deployments or proxy configurations.

entity.title: Cache
entity.description: The cache configuration in config.yaml that controls local storage of resolved rules and evaluation results.

rules.body: MAY have a `ttl` property of type string.

entity.title: Fail Conditions
entity.description: The fail_on configuration in config.yaml that determines which rule lifecycle states trigger enforcement failures.

rules.body: Each item MUST be a valid rule lifecycle state code: Draft (D), Abandoned (A), Published (P), Implemented (I), Retired (R), Terminated (T).

notes.content: Typical configuration: `fail_on: [I]` fails only on Implemented rule violations. Stricter: `fail_on: [P, I]` also fails on Published rules. The array references rule lifecycle states defined by RULST.
The `.strspc/` prefix follows the dotfile convention for tool configuration (cf. `.git/`, `.vscode/`). The name derives from the SteerSpec project abbreviation.
A source can reference a remote Realm (`github://SteerSpec/strspc-rules@v1/rules/core`) or a local directory (`./rules/`). At least one must be declared for the configuration to be meaningful.
A project with both `.strspc/config.yaml` and `rules/realm.json` is simultaneously a rule consumer and a rule author. The config references the local Realm via `source: ./rules/`.

A rule defines what must be true. How critical a violation is depends on the consuming project's context. The same rule might be a hard failure for a fintech API and a warning for an internal tool. This separation prevents rule authors from imposing enforcement policy on consumers (Rule Manager Spec §1.1).
